# Pr 129874 Timebox Abort Idempotency Resume Openai Compat

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_9550250a59e74f1bbd5612480b2e7100
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
introduce-server-timebox-and-idempotent-cancel-fallbacks-for-streaming-compat

Context: production customers observed cases where long lived streaming sessions (tool calls + structured output) were left in indeterminate states after a client network disconnect or a client-initiated Cancel. Motivation: we need a predictable server-side safety net that bounds resource residency time, ensures tool-call idempotency when retries/resume occur, and provides a clear fallback path (trimmed response + final event) that OpenAI-compatible clients can rely on. This PR implements a server-side streaming timebox (configurable per-route) that gracefully converts oversized/inflight streams into a final 'timeboxed' terminal event, augments client-cancel handling to be idempotent across reconnect/resume windows, and plumbs a compact resume token that includes tool-call idempotency metadata. Summary of changes: 1) stream manager: add configurable timebox_ms and a deterministic finalization path that emits a structured terminal event ("__redwood_timeboxed__") with a compact summary; 2) openai adapter: interpret Cancel events as idempotent markers tied to resume tokens; retries within the resume window will deduplicate tool calls using a request-level id; 3) tooling: map function-call invocations to idempotency keys and persist minimal call metadata to the stream checkpoint to support safe resume; 4) API: extend the OpenAI-compat streaming spec with two optional fields in the final event (timeboxed=true, resume_hint) without breaking existing clients; 5) tests & docs: integration tests for flaky network simulated disconnects and release notes for the behavior and recommended operational knobs. Rollout plan: feature guarded behind a runtime flag and route-level config. Backwards compatibility: default behavior is unchanged unless a route opts-in. Checklist: [x] unit tests for stream finalizer [x] integration test that simulates disconnect + resume [x] docs page and compatibility notes [x] change log entry. Related: ENG-1023 (stream lifecycle SLO), SUP-4572 (customer incident replicant).
stream: add timebox lifecycle and finalizer logic (runtime)
compat: interpret cancel as idempotent marker with resume token (openai adapter)
stream: persist minimal tool-call idempotency metadata to checkpoints
api: add 'timeboxed' and 'resume_hint' flags to streaming final event schema
tests: integration tests for disconnect/resume/timebox behavior
docs: add streaming-lifecycle/timebox operational runbook
Ava Chen (2026-02-19): Nice approach. Two asks: 1) can we make the default timebox conservative (eg 30s) and 2) add metrics for timebox-triggered finalizations so SRE can track this?
Marcus Li (2026-02-19): Thanks Ava. Default is 30s in the flags; added metric 'stream.timebox_finalized' with labels route/model; will push change.
Diego Martinez (2026-02-20): Concern about persisting tool-call metadata on every request path. How big can the checkpoint grow? What retention semantics?
Marcus Li (2026-02-20): Checkpoints store only a 64-bit idempotency hash + 3-field small metadata (call name, timestamp, status). We GC per-checkpoint on successful terminalization. Size is bounded and measured in tests.
Priya Nair (2026-02-21): Integration test flaky on first run, failure in 'resume-after-disconnect' when the simulated retry arrives slightly after timebox expiry. Can we make the test deterministic?
Marcus Li (2026-02-21): Fixed by injecting a deterministic clock to the stream finalizer and making the resume window configurable for tests. Reran CI locally.
SRE Team (CI bot) (2026-02-24): One lint failure in openai_compat_stream.go for an unused variable — fixed in follow-up commit.
Ava Chen (2026-02-24): Approved after last fixes. Marking as ready to merge.
Server-side streaming timebox (route-configurable) that guarantees a graceful terminal event when streams exceed a configured residency time. Client Cancel events are now idempotent within a short resume window; resume tokens include minimal idempotency hints to avoid duplicated tool/function executions on reconnect. Default behavior unchanged unless enabled per-route.
